body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
<!--- One sentence to describe the purpose of this PR, refer to any linked issues:
#14 -- this will link to issue 14
Closes #2 -- this will automatically close issue 2 on PR merge
-->
This PR adds a call to `update_aux` at the end of `init_tc!`, in order to populate `Field`s that are required for diagnostics. This is required for #1376, since that PR sets the `aux` state to `NaN` in order to ensure consistency. It is a step toward closing #1389.

## Content
- Removes the modification of the rain and snow tendencies from `update_aux!`. Now, the rain and snow tendencies will be stored in the `aux` state, along with the tendencies of energy and total moisture due to precipitation.
- Changes the initial conditions so that the rain and snow prognostic variables are only allocated for 1-moment microphysics, which closes #1388.
- *Note*: The rain and snow tendencies are still being computed for models with no precipitation or with 0-moment microphysics; it's just that now they will be stored in the `aux` state instead of being used to modify the net tendency of the prognostic state. In the future, we should eliminate these unused computations altogether.

- checked that this PR does not duplicate an open PR.

In the Content, I have included 
- relevant unit tests, and integration tests, 
- appropriate docstrings on all functions, structs, and modules, and included relevant documentation.

-->

- [x] I have read and checked the items on the review checklist.
title: Call update_aux at the end of init_tc!

body:
This PR adds a base flux test for orographic gravity wave. It follows the setup of figure 1 in the Garner05 paper.

closes #1378
title: add base flux test for the orographic gravity wave

body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
<!--- One sentence to describe the purpose of this PR, refer to any linked issues:
#14 -- this will link to issue 14
Closes #2 -- this will automatically close issue 2 on PR merge
-->
This PR simplifies `init_tc!` in order to facilitate the debugging of the TRMM case in #1327. Following the bug fixes in #1356 and #1327, this should not change any of the CI cases. Since `init_tc!` is going to be removed in #1327, the specifics of this simplified implementation don't really matter.

## Content
<!---  specific tasks that are currently complete 
- Solution implemented
-->
- Move all of the code from `set_thermo_state_pθq!` and `set_grid_mean_from_thermo_state!` to `init_tc!`, and remove those functions from `dycore.jl`.
- Move all of the code from `initialize_edmf` (and, by extension, `initialize_updrafts` and `initialize_covariance`) to `init_tc!`, and delete `initial_conditions.jl`.
- Remove all unnecessary manipulations of auxiliary variables from `init_tc!`, and remove the call to `assign_thermo_aux!`.
- Add a sanity check to the beginning of `init_tc!` that sets all uninitialized auxiliary variables to `NaN`.
- Replace the uninitialized auxiliary variable `aux_gm.q_liq` with `0` in `ARM_SGP`'s `initialize_profiles` method.
- Clearly label every prognostic variable being manipulated in `init_tc!`, and ensure that it is only set once.

<!---

title: Simplify init_tc! to facilitate debugging
